title: juncus balticus subsp. pyrenaeus
intro: Juncus balticus subsp. pyrenaeus, also known as rush or sea rush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae, characterized by its narrow leaves and delicate white flowers. The medicinal properties of Juncus balticus subsp. pyrenaeus include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues. Juncus balticus subsp. pyrenaeus can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
